Title: Global landscape of phenazine biosynthesis and biodegradation reveals species-specific colonization patterns in agricultural soils and crop microbiomes

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates PCA biodegradation; phenazine-degrading bacteria were rare but detectable in some rhizospheres, especially switchgrass.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: metagenomic abundance mining in simulated and environmental datasets using reference gene mapping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phdA was used as a marker for phenazine-degrading bacteria. 2. The approach accurately estimated phdA+ degrader abundance in simulated communities. 3. Substantial phdA+ signals were uncommon in environmental samples and were enriched mainly in switchgrass rhizospheres.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradation of antimicrobial phenazines in soil microbiomes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker to monitor phenazine turnover capacity in crop microbiomes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: shotgun metagenomics; SRA BioProject PRJNA634917 plus public SRA datasets
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May counter beneficial phenazine-mediated biocontrol if abundant
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states phdA gene product begins PCA degradation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker for pyocyanin biodegradation; detected only rarely in soil and plant-associated microbiomes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: metagenomic abundance mining in simulated and environmental datasets using reference gene mapping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. podA was included as a phenazine biodegradation marker. 2. Simulations showed abundance could be estimated quantitatively. 3. podA evidence was scarce in the surveyed environments.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ecological marker for pyocyanin-degrading microbiomes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: shotgun metagenomics; SRA BioProject PRJNA634917 plus public SRA datasets
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could reduce phenazine persistence if present
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study found scarce evidence for podA in agricultural soils and crop microbiomes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: UNC79MFTsu3.2; Sugar Buns F1 for maize colonization context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core phenazine biosynthesis leading to PDC-derived phenazines
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Core phenazine scaffold precursor leading to phenazine-1,6-dicarboxylic acid (PDC)-derived phenazines
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Core catalytic component of the conserved phenazine biosynthetic pathway required to form the phenazine scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of terminal phenazine products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial metabolites with potential crop-protective activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes orange pigment formation and eliminates detectable phenazines in D. japonica.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: clean deletion mutant; LC-MS metabolite profiling; pigment phenotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzA is required for phenazine production in D. japonica. 2. ΔphzA cultures lost orange pigmentation. 3. ΔphzA lacked PDC, myxin, and reduced myxin derivative detected in WT by LC-MS.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Phenazine production increases under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: phosphate limitation (1 mM versus 50 mM phosphate) induces phenazine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: phzB; phzD; phzE; phzF; phzG; nearby modification genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: shotgun metagenomics PRJNA634917; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Phenazine production had a slight adverse effect on maize mono-colonization relative to mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering or selecting phenazine-producing biocontrol strains
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focused on phzA as a genetically tractable representative of the core phz operon in D. japonica.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Streptomyces sp. WAC04657 (homologous annotation discussed); function cited from Pseudomonas
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies phenazine precursor to alternate terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of a phzH-homolog in a dominant Streptomyces group suggests potential PCN production in the wheat rhizosphere.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomic region analysis from metagenomic mapping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A phzH-homolog was identified in Streptomyces sp. WAC04657-like genomes enriched in wheat rhizosphere metagenomes. 2. The homolog is inferred to convert PCA to PCN based on similarity to Pseudomonas phzH. 3. This supports chemical diversification of rhizosphere phenazines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can inform selection of strains producing specific phenazine derivatives for biocontrol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This function is inferred from homology and genomic context, not directly biochemically validated in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: UNC79MFTsu3.2; Sugar Buns F1 for maize assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative transcription factor adjacent to phenazine biosynthetic operon
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation of phenazine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: adjacent phenazine biosynthesis operon phzA/BDEFG and associated modification genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphate limitation is associated with induction of phenazine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial metabolites with potential agricultural biocontrol activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of phzT abolishes visible pigment and eliminates detectable phenazines, showing it is essential for phenazine biosynthesis in D. japonica.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: clean deletion mutant; HPLC-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; pigment phenotype; maize colonization assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phzT encodes a conserved transcription factor adjacent to the D. japonica phenazine operon. 2. ΔphzT mutants are phenazine-deficient and lack PDC, myxin, and reduced myxin derivative in LC-MS analyses. 3. phzT is therefore an essential positive regulator of phenazine biosynthesis. 4. ΔphzT showed slightly higher maize root colonization than WT in mono-colonization assays.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of D. japonica phenazine production
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: phosphate limitation induces phenazine biosynthesis; maize gnotobiotic seedling assay for colonization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not directly measured; inferred activity increases under phosphate limitation because phenazine output rises
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Phenazine production causes a slight colonization cost in mono-colonization assay; ΔphzT colonized maize roots ~1.5-fold better than WT
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory target for tuning phenazine output in agricultural inoculants while balancing colonization fitness
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: shotgun metagenomics PRJNA634917; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper denotes N515DRAFT_3255 as phzT; TF family was not specified.
